abstract:
The rapid maturation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the proliferation of internet-connected sensory devices have transformed the traditional educational environment into the "Smart Classroom." This paper provides a comprehensive review of the technological landscape of these modern learning spaces, with a specific focus on the integration of AI-driven emerging technologies. We categorize the current state of the art into three functional pillars: classroom management (including computer vision for attendance and automated behavioral surveillance), advanced teaching aids (specifically educational robotics, chatbots, and immersive VR/AR/MR environments), and automated performance assessment for both students and educators. 

Beyond cataloging these advancements, the study offers a critical evaluation of the impact of AI on pedagogy, highlighting how these tools facilitate personalized and adaptive learning experiences. Through a detailed SWOT analysis, we identify the internal strengths and weaknesses of adopting AI in the classroom, alongside the external opportunities and threats—such as data privacy concerns, algorithmic bias, and the necessity for educator re-skilling. The paper concludes by outlining future research directions, emphasizing the need for low-cost, interoperable infrastructure and ethical data-handling frameworks to ensure that the next generation of smart learning environments is both effective and equitable. This survey serves as a strategic roadmap for both educators seeking to understand technological potential and AI professionals aiming to address the unique constraints of the educational sector.
